findings.title: Add Notification Priority Levels
findings.description: Implement a tiered notification priority system (urgent, normal, low) to help users distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates.
assumptions: Users need a clear way to differentiate notification urgency.

evidence.excerpt: Users need a way to distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates.

recommendations: Develop a visual design for priority levels.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user receives notifications WHEN they view their notification center THEN notifications should display with appropriate priority levels (urgent, normal, low).

findings.title: Implement Intelligent Notification Filtering
findings.description: Create a feature that intelligently filters notifications based on user engagement data to reduce irrelevant alerts.
assumptions: Users are willing to pay for improved notification filtering.

evidence.excerpt: Power users express a willingness to pay for a feature that intelligently filters notifications.

recommendations: Conduct user testing to refine filtering criteria.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user receives notifications WHEN the filtering feature is enabled THEN only relevant notifications based on user engagement should be displayed.

findings.title: Implement Notification Digest Summaries
findings.description: Batch low-priority notifications into a digest format to be delivered at optimal times based on user engagement patterns.
assumptions: Users prefer receiving low-priority notifications in digest form.

evidence.excerpt: Casual users prefer receiving low-priority notifications in digest form.

recommendations: Analyze user engagement data to optimize digest timing.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user has low-priority notifications WHEN the digest feature is enabled THEN they should receive a summary of these notifications at optimal times.

findings.title: Add User Preference Page for Notification Categories
findings.description: Allow users to opt in/out of specific notification categories (marketing, product updates, etc.) from a settings page.
assumptions: Users want control over the types of notifications they receive.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Ensure the preferences API respects global mute settings.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the preferences page WHEN they select notification categories THEN their preferences should be saved and respected in the notification system.

findings.title: Reduce Notification Delivery Latency
findings.description: Optimize the notification delivery system to reduce p95 latency from 340ms to under 200ms.
assumptions: Reducing latency will improve user experience.

evidence.excerpt: Performance monitoring shows p95 notification delivery latency at 340ms.

recommendations: Implement asynchronous writes and batch inserts for analytics.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the notification delivery system is optimized WHEN notifications are sent THEN the p95 delivery latency should be under 200ms.

findings.title: Improve Accessibility of Notification Center
findings.description: Enhance the notification center to ensure it is navigable by users with screen readers and other assistive technologies.
assumptions: Accessibility improvements will benefit users with disabilities.

evidence.excerpt: Several users, particularly those using screen readers, face challenges navigating the notification center.

recommendations: Conduct accessibility testing with users who rely on assistive technologies.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user with a screen reader accesses the notification center WHEN they navigate through notifications THEN all notifications should be accessible and properly announced.

findings.title: Conduct User Feedback Collection on Notification Features
findings.description: Gather user feedback on the new notification features to assess satisfaction and areas for improvement.
assumptions: User feedback is essential for iterative improvement.
recommendations: Create a feedback form integrated into the app.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user has access to the new notification features WHEN they provide feedback THEN their responses should be collected and analyzed for future improvements.

findings.title: Monitor Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR)
findings.description: Implement monitoring for the notification click-through rate to evaluate the effectiveness of notifications.
assumptions: Tracking CTR will help improve notification relevance.

evidence.excerpt: The notification click-through rate (CTR) measures the percentage of notifications that users interact with.

recommendations: Set up dashboards to visualize CTR trends.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the notification system is live WHEN notifications are sent THEN the CTR should be tracked and reported in real-time.

findings.title: Track Average Notifications per User
findings.description: Implement tracking for the average number of notifications received by each user to understand exposure and fatigue.
assumptions: Understanding notification exposure will help mitigate fatigue.

evidence.excerpt: This metric tracks the average number of notifications received by each user.

recommendations: Integrate tracking into the existing analytics framework.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the notification system is operational WHEN users receive notifications THEN the average notifications per user should be calculated and reported.

findings.title: Monitor Unsubscribe Rate
findings.description: Implement monitoring for the unsubscribe rate to identify potential notification fatigue or irrelevance.
assumptions: Tracking unsubscribe rates will help identify issues with notification relevance.

evidence.excerpt: The unsubscribe rate indicates the percentage of users opting out of notifications.

recommendations: Create alerts for significant changes in unsubscribe rates.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the notification system is live WHEN users unsubscribe from notifications THEN the unsubscribe rate should be tracked and reported.

findings.title: Optimize Notification Timing Based on User Engagement
findings.description: Conduct A/B testing on different notification timing strategies to determine optimal delivery times based on user engagement patterns.
assumptions: Optimizing notification timing will improve CTR and reduce unsubscribe rates.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct A/B testing on different notification timing strategies.

recommendations: Analyze A/B test results to refine notification timing.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN different notification timing strategies are tested WHEN users receive notifications THEN the optimal timing should be identified based on engagement metrics.

summary: The analysis of customer insights and metrics findings has led to the creation of a comprehensive backlog for the SmartNotify product. Key requirements include implementing notification priority levels, intelligent filtering, digest summaries, and accessibility improvements. Additionally, metrics monitoring for user engagement and unsubscribe rates is prioritized to enhance user experience and reduce notification fatigue.
